mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate directly increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with t-stat > 2.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous peaks in auto sales and housing starts indicate maximum household leverage and demand exhaustion, which mechanically forces a subsequent drawdown in equity markets over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint peaks in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of historical cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly boosts corporate revenue and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the economy operates above potential output.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during supply-constrained periods, with mean forward return <= 0.0% and p-value > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing rapid equity sell-offs over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly prints above 0.4% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or signal hit rate drops below 50% out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand without overheating, supporting positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, triggering negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO MoM changes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.
